返回本批次

收到 DMCA 通知后，媒体抓取工具 Gallery-dl 将迁移到 Codeberg

Media scraper Gallery-dl is moving to Codeberg after receiving a DMCA notice

github.com 作者 MoltenMonster 分数 129 评论 38 2026/04/06 11:28
打开 HN 原帖 打开原始文章

中文摘要

  1. Fakku 过去和将来都是由恶棍组成的。创始人 Jacob 创建了 Fakku，因为他看到 Exhentai 的受欢迎程度和盈利能力不断增长。 Jacob 最初创建 Fakku 作为 Exhentai 刮刀。在广告商帮助他之前，他用学生贷款支付了网站的账单。他有一家商店，出售装饰有受版权保护的色情内容的物品，我怀疑他是否获得了许可。 Fakku 试图通过告知日本公司 Wanimagazine Exhentai 正在分享他们杂志的纸质扫描件来从 Exhentai 挖走用户。 Fakku 是如此可悲，以至于人们立刻就知道告密者就是 Fakku，而且由于 Fakku 是一名 Exhentai 刮刀，Wanimagazine 还威胁 Fakku 删除扫描结果，Fakku 将其编成假证据，证明他们不可能是告密者。 Exhentai 上的一个模组透露，Wanimagazine 给了 Exhentai 和 Fakku 相同的协议：成为他们的员工，永远不分享未经许可的内容。 Exhentai 拒绝了，Fakku 接受了。 Fakku 在与 Wanimagazine 合作期间继续分享未经许可的内容大约一年，直到 Wanimagazine 再次向他们发出最后通牒后才停止。大约在这个时候，Jacob 公开发布了 Exhentai 所有者的恶搞，揭露 Fakku 想要秘密审查内容。雅各布开始试图向所有人保证，他将 Fakku 创建为 Exhentai 刮刀，以建立足够的人气以说服 Wanimagazine 雇用他。哦，我记得我问雅各布他和他的员工是否正在使用授权的 Adob​​e 产品，他拒绝回答，并且对这个问题感到非常恼火。当 Fakku 试图为他们对言论自由的攻击辩护时，他们总是会试图用谎言来操纵你，说他们支持言论自由，在经济上支持每个艺术家，未经授权的复制是盗窃，版权声明或“DMCAing”不是审查制度。当他们发现他们的废话对你不起作用时，他们会承认他们只是想压迫你，因为他们“合法”可以这样做。 Fakku 将已故艺术家的内容放在付费墙后面。当 Fakku 在一年内获得七位数的利润时，他们恳求人们捐赠 4.7 万美元用于授权翻译。如果你捐款超过 2500 美元，雅各布承诺会在东京与你和艺术家见面，但你必须支付路费。考虑到他们想要翻译的内容，很明显他们是在乞求捐款，因为他们不相信它会卖出很多份。 Fakku 在 2021 年的 11 个月内向谷歌发送了超过 4500 万条版权删除请求，他还向记者和新闻网站发送了此类请求，试图审查他们报道他对言论自由的攻击。 Fakku只关心金钱并且强迫性地撒谎，因此Fakku拒绝采取原则立场。 Irodori Comics 更频繁地重复与 Fakku 相同的反分享谎言。 Fakku 不允许任何人免费使用他们的商店，这就是为什么当人们得知 Irodori Comics 通过未经所有者许可翻译流行作品、在大型集会中发布 Nhentai URL 以及帮助为更多未经许可的翻译筹集捐款而获得报酬时，他们拒绝禁止 Irodori Comics。 Fakku 和 Irodori Comics 都不出售他们审查过的大多数作品的翻译版。这是因为，除了享受审查内容之外，他们还获得艺术家的报酬，以代表艺术家发送版权删除请求。 Irodori Comics 实际上可能是 Fakku 的代理。 Fakku 讨厌与低质量的翻译、排版和刻字联系在一起。一名提供临时工作的人和一名前雇员都表示，Irodori Comics 的工资远低于最低工资，这解释了为什么当他们发布授权翻译时，其中包含如此多的错误。 Irodori Comics 已经审查了 Exhentai 上可能多达 100000 个画廊。第一批允许 Irodori Comics 翻译其自行出版作品的艺术家曾受雇于或曾受雇于 Wanimagazine。在 Edsel Ayes（又名 EdMX）“共同创立”Irodori Comics 几年前，Jacob 就为他在 Fakku 上建立了一个博客。我无法证实这个谣言，但这听起来是 Fakku 和 Irodori Comics 的典型。他们不会免费翻译或出售艺术家的作品，并且在售出足够的副本之前不会与艺术家分享部分利润。他们显然对真正受欢迎的艺术家例外。

Original Summary

  1. Fakku was and will always be made up of villains. The founder, Jacob, created Fakku because he saw Exhentai's growing popularity and profitability. Jacob originally created Fakku as an Exhentai scraper. Before advertisers helped him, he covered the site's bills with student loans. He had a store where he sold items adorned with copyrighted porn that I doubt he had permission for. Fakku tried poaching users from Exhentai by informing Wanimagazine, a Japanese corporation, that Exhentai is sharing paper scans of their magazines. Fakku is so deplorable that people immediately knew the snitch was Fakku, and since Fakku was an Exhentai scraper, Wanimagazine also threatened Fakku to remove the scans, and Fakku spun it into fake evidence that they couldn't have been the snitch because of it. A mod on Exhentai revealed that Wanimagazine gave Exhentai and Fakku the same deal: become their employee and never share unlicensed content. Exhentai declined and Fakku accepted. Fakku continued sharing unlicensed content for about another year while working with Wanimagazine, stopping only after Wanimagazine gave them one more ultimatum. Around this time, Jacob publicly posted his dox of Exhentai's owner for revealing that Fakku wants to secretly censor content. Jacob began trying to assure everyone that he created Fakku as an Exhentai scraper to build enough popularity to convince Wanimagazine to hire him. Oh, I remember asking Jacob if he and his employees are using licensed Adobe products, he refused to answer and he was very annoyed by the question. When Fakku tries to justify their attack on free speech, they'll always try to manipulate you with lies by saying they support free speech, support every artist financially, an unauthorized copy is theft, and copyright claiming or "DMCAing" isn't censorship. When they see their bullshit isn't working on you, they'll admit that they're only trying to oppress you because they "legally" can. Fakku puts content from dead artists behind their paywall. When Fakku made a seven figure profit in a year, they begged people to donate $47k for a licensed translation. If you donated more than $2500, Jacob promised to meet up with you and the artist in Tokyo, but you had to pay for the trip. Given what they wanted to translate, it’s clear they were begging for donations because they didn’t believe it would sell many copies. Fakku sent Google over 45 million copyright removal requests in 11 months in 2021 and he has sent such requests to journalists and news sites to try and censor them for covering his attack on free speech. Fakku only cares about money and lies compulsively, hence why Fakku refuses to take a principled stance. Irodori Comics regurgitates the same anti-sharing lies as Fakku at a much greater frequency. Fakku doesn't let anyone use their store for free and that's why they refused to ban Irodori Comics when people learned Irodori Comics get paid to translate popular works without the owners' permission, post Nhentai URLs in large gatherings, and help solicit donations for more unlicensed translations. Neither Fakku nor Irodori Comics sell translations of most of the works they’ve censored. This is because, besides enjoying censoring content, they get paid by the artist to send a copyright removal request on the artist's behalf. Irodori Comics might actually be a proxy of Fakku. Fakku hates being associated with low quality translating, typesetting and lettering. One person offered a temporary job and a former employee both said Irodori Comics pay far below minimum wage, which explains why when they release a licensed translation, it contains so many errors. Irodori Comics has censored possibly up to 100000 galleries on Exhentai. The first artists who allowed their self-published works to be translated by Irodori Comics were employed or previously employed by Wanimagazine. Years before Edsel Ayes aka EdMX "co-founded" Irodori Comics, Jacob set up a blog on Fakku for him. I haven't been able to verify this rumor but it sounds typical of Fakku and Irodori Comics. They won't translate or sell an artist's work for free and they don't share some of the profit with the artist until they've sold enough copies. They apparently make exceptions for really popular artists.

评论（默认中文）

miyuru 2026/04/06 16:20
线程中有一个屏幕截图，一切都有意义。

> 我会告诉我们的 DMCA 代理，您已经安全了

https://github.com/mikf/gallery-dl/discussions/9304#discussi...

与专利流氓类似，现在也有 DMCA 流氓可供雇用。

There is a screenshot in the thread that makes all make sense.

> I'll tell our DMCA agent you're in the clear

https://github.com/mikf/gallery-dl/discussions/9304#discussi...

similar to patent trolls, there is now a DMCA trolls for hire.

在 HN 中查看
ronsor 2026/04/06 11:57
重要的是要记住，这些项目并不违反版权法，也不是规避工具，针对它们提交 DMCA 通知实际上是非法的。
It's important to remember that these projects are not violating copyright law, are not circumvention tools, and that filing a DMCA notice against them is in fact unlawful.
在 HN 中查看
sammy2255 2026/04/06 12:51
但没有人有勇气、时间或金钱去挑战它
No one has the guts, time, or money to challenge it though
在 HN 中查看
userbinator 2026/04/06 12:59
这就是像 EFF 这样的团体的目的： https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_litigation_involving_t...
This is what groups like the EFF are for: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_litigation_involving_t...
在 HN 中查看
ddtaylor 2026/04/06 13:05
可悲的是，你基本上是对的，评论部分说寻找无偿律师是可笑的。我认为任何相信存在这种现象的人都应该真正接触到 真实的 律师，看看谈话进展如何。我曾经有过这样的谈话。

首先，他们在大多数情况下都不存在，你不能真正打电话给律师并与他们交谈——你得到他们的办公室，他们的“工作”就是阻止律师与任何没有代表或没有付费咨询的人进行任何讨论。

其次，一旦你与他们取得联系，你会得到一个茫然的眼神或电话沉默。这并不是大多数律师对公益工作的看法。他们中的大多数人只需要完成很少的公益工作，仅此而已。他们的公司会分配一个案件，或者每年从州政府那里接受一些案件，然后他们就完成了。如今，认为无私的律师愿意从事免费和无偿工作的想法几乎不存在。

Sadly, you're mostly right and the comments section saying to find a pro-bono lawyer is laughable. I think anyone who believes that exists should actually reach out to a real lawyer and see how that conversation goes. I've had those conversations.

Firstly, they can't exist most of the time you can't actually call a lawyer and talk to them - you get their office and their "job" is to gatekeep that lawyer from making any discussions with anyone who isn't represented or paid for a consultation.

Secondly, once you do get into contact with them you'll get a blank stare or phone silence. This is not how most lawyers view pro-bono work. Most of them have a very small quota of pro-bono work to be done and that's it. They get assigned a case by their firm or go and accept a few a year from the state and they're done with it. The idea that an altruistic lawyer exists out there ready to do free and unpaid work is virtually non-existent today.

在 HN 中查看
direwolf20 2026/04/06 15:28
如果您真诚地相信它是一种规避工具，那么它就是合法的。

这甚至可能是真的。没有下载按钮是 DMCA 中定义的一种版权保护措施。如果该项目绕过了没有下载按钮的情况，则属于 DMCA 规定的非法规避措施。

It's lawful if you have a good faith belief that it's a circumvention tool.

It might even be true. Not having a download button is a copy protection measure as defined in the DMCA. If this project bypasses not having a download button, it's an illegal circumvention measure under DMCA.

在 HN 中查看
dns_snek 2026/04/06 15:42
> 没有下载按钮是一种版权保护措施。

这太荒谬了。没有某事与积极采取措施预防某事不同。我可以类似地提出这样的论点：我可以在设备上观看的任何内容实际上都没有复制保护措施，因为这些字节是有目的地复制到我的显示缓冲区中的。

反规避条款是一种必须消灭的癌症。它们可以用来将任何事物定为犯罪。

> Not having a download button is a copy protection measure.

That's absurd. Not having something is different from actively implementing measures to prevent something. I could similarly make the argument that any content that I can watch on my device doesn't really have copy protection measures because those bytes were purposefully copied into my display buffer.

Anti-circumvention provisions are a cancer that needs to die. They can be used to criminalize just about anything.

在 HN 中查看
ehhthing 2026/04/06 15:32
这里的问题是，投诉似乎是由版权所有者（或被许可人）提出的，但代码正在访问盗版网站。如果盗版网站提出版权投诉，则可能存在规避案件，但他们没有。
The problem here is that the complaint seems to be filed by the copyright owner (or licensee) but the code is accessing piracy sites. There could be a circumvention case if the piracy site is the one filing the copyright complaint, but they have not.
在 HN 中查看
close04 2026/04/06 15:58
这是基准吗？禁用右键单击以防止访问者保存内容的网站仍然可以由浏览器保存。这是一种禁止浏览器绕过下载的主动措施。那么 Chrome 会下降吗？
Is that the benchmark? A website that disables the right click to prevent visitors from saving the content can still be saved by the browser. That’s an active measure to disable downloads being circumvented by the browser. So is Chrome going down?
在 HN 中查看
imrozim 2026/04/06 12:39 deleted
[死的]
[dead]
在 HN 中查看
charcircuit 2026/04/06 12:04
对我来说，链接到其内容都是公然窃取艺术家作品的盗版网站似乎确实违反了规定。
Linking to piracy sites whose content is all blatantly stolen from artists does seem violating to me.
在 HN 中查看
ddtaylor 2026/04/06 12:52
这似乎是通过适当的 DMCA 删除请求追查实际涉嫌非法托管的材料的论据。
That seems like an argument to go after the actual alleged illegally hosted materials through the proper DMCA takedown request.
在 HN 中查看
charcircuit 2026/04/06 12:54
两者都应该做。通常，实际非法托管的材料位于对删除请求不友好的服务器上，或者会立即被盗版者重新加载。通过追踪链接，它可以切断人们找到非法托管材料的能力。
Both should be done. Often the actual illegally hosted materials are on servers not friendly with takedown requests or will get immediately reloaded by the pirates. By going after the links it can cut off the ability for people to find the illegally hosted materials.
在 HN 中查看
ddtaylor 2026/04/06 13:02
试图通过代理来监管互联网似乎是一种奇怪的方式。互联网应该忽略或绕开那些试图监管节点如何相互连接的人。
Seems like a strange way to attempt to police the internet by proxy. The Internet should ignore or route around people attempting to police how nodes connect to each other.
在 HN 中查看
direwolf20 2026/04/06 15:32
这是否像法国一样，法律要求 DNS 解析器阻止某些网站？没错，如果你在法国使用默认选项运行“unbound”，那么你就是重罪犯。
Is this like how in France, DNS resolvers are legally required to block certain websites? That's right, if you run "unbound" with default options in France you're a felon.

raziel2701 2026/04/06 12:22
gallery-dl 链接到哪些盗版网站？
what piracy sites is gallery-dl linking to?
在 HN 中查看
charcircuit 2026/04/06 12:30
我不想在这里宣传它们，但如果您阅读链接的 github 线程，您将看到删除的提取器的名称。
I do not want to promote them here, but if you read the linked github thread you will see the names of what extractors were deleted.
在 HN 中查看
ddtaylor 2026/04/06 13:08
等一下...根据你现在所拥护的观点，这难道不意味着 这 谈话“非法”？为什么我们不向 HN 提交 DMCA 删除，因为顽皮网站列表位于该线程的页面顶部？

这看起来就像乌龟一路向下。

Wait a second... By the view you're espousing right now, doesn't that make this conversation "illegal"? Why aren't we filing DMCA takedowns to HN because the list of the naughty sites is at the top of the page for this very thread?

This seems like turtles all the way down.

在 HN 中查看
logifail 2026/04/06 12:55
通过DCMA镜头，是否有一个工具能够从example.com下载= 链接到 示例.com？
Through the DCMA lens, does a tool having the ability to download from example.com = linking to example.com?
在 HN 中查看
charcircuit 2026/04/06 13:00
在这种情况下，您可以在 github 上查看的文件实际上具有直接指向受版权保护作品的链接。这不仅仅是因为它与盗版网站兼容。
In this case the files you could view on github literally had links directly to copyrighted works. It was not just that it was compatible with pirate sites.
在 HN 中查看
KomoD 2026/04/06 13:16
在哪里？我查看了 3 月 16 日的副本，在问题中提到的文件中只看到了 12345 和 12345/67890abcde 等占位符
Where? I looked at a copy from March 16, and I only saw placeholders like 12345 and 12345/67890abcde in the files mentioned in the issue
在 HN 中查看
charcircuit 2026/04/06 13:19
查看测试向量，您将看到那些不适用于通用 id 的向量。
Look in the test vectors and you will see ones that are not for generic ids.
在 HN 中查看
ddtaylor 2026/04/06 12:59
最近，随着版权滥用的增加和人工智能模糊了界限，越来越多的人将参与去中心化系统，这让我感到兴奋。

有多种不同的方法可以通过 DHT 网络（例如 BitTorrent）托管 git 存储库。同样，也有一些方法可以为 apt 等 Linux 包管理器运行 DHT 支持的命令。

这些工具通常很少受到好评，因为当中心化系统对大多数用户来说没有太多问题时，去中心化系统的价值似乎很低。

最近，这种狂热化的速度如此之快，以至于越来越多的人向我求助，询问如何设置 Linux 系统、家庭媒体服务器等。我真的很喜欢这些技术，但在过去的十年里，我或多或少只是对它们闭嘴，以避免成为那样的人。

It's exciting to me recently with the increase in copyright abuse and AI blurring the lines that more people are going to be involved with decentralized systems.

There have been multiple different ways to host git repositories over DHT networks such as BitTorrent. Similarly there have been ways to run DHT backed commands for Linux package managers like apt.

These tools often receive little praise because the value of decentralized systems seems low when centralized systems are working to most users without too many issues.

The enshittification is ramping up so quickly recently that more people are reaching out to me on how to setup Linux syatems, home media servers, etc. I genuinely enjoy these technologies, but for the last decade I had more or less just shut up about them to avoid being that guy.

在 HN 中查看
14 2026/04/06 13:10
我实际上只是想知道种子是否是正确的选择。 我没有任何使用 github 的经验，所以不确定 torrent 是否完全合适，但我一直认为它们是去中心化的，所以一旦发布就很难停止，只要有人拥有副本。
I was actually just wondering if torrents were the way to go. I don't have any experience with github so not sure if torrents are at all suitable but I always had the thought that they were decentralized so once released hard to stop as long as someone has a copy.

ernsheong 2026/04/06 12:15

They can just file another DMCA against Codeberg, what am I missing here?

normie3000 2026/04/06 12:24
听起来 Codeberg 仍会删除该存储库，但会提供更多支持： https://blog.codeberg.org/on-the-youtube-dl-dmca-takedown.ht... (2020)

也许还有更多最近的例子？

Sounds like Codeberg would still take down the repo, but would be more supportive: https://blog.codeberg.org/on-the-youtube-dl-dmca-takedown.ht... (2020)

Maybe there are more recent examples?

landr0id 2026/04/06 12:50

huijzer 2026/04/06 13:09

Or self-host Forgejo?
在 HN 中查看
JLO64 2026/04/06 13:36
自从任天堂在 GitHub 上追踪 Eden Switch 模拟器以来，这就是 Eden Switch 模拟器所做的事情。
This is what the Eden switch emulator does ever since Nintendo went after them on GitHub.
在 HN 中查看
notThrowingAway 2026/04/06 12:25
这并不容易，因为 Codeberg 遵循德国法律。
That won't be easy because Codeberg follows German law.
在 HN 中查看
echelon 2026/04/06 12:41
如果足够大的参与者提出 DMCA 请求或诉讼，德国和欧盟可能会向美国屈服。

大资金利益与美国政客擦肩而过，美国政客也与海外政客打交道。因此，足够大的 DMCA 请求将与国际贸易和其他地缘政治问题同时被秘密提及。在足够亲密的朋友和盟友之间的交易中，金钱可以保护金钱。

另一方面，如果 Codeberg 的总部位于俄罗斯或美国的地缘政治对手，此类请求可能会被忽略。

Germany and the EU will probably kowtow to the US if the DMCA requests or lawsuits are brought by big enough players.

Big money interests rub shoulders with US politicians, US politicians deal with their overseas counterparts. Therefore, big enough DMCA requests will be mentioned behind closed doors in the same breath as international trade and other geopolitical concerns. Money protects money in deals between close enough friends and allies.

If Codeberg were based in Russia or a US geopolitical adversary, on the other hand, such requests would likely be ignored.

在 HN 中查看
lmz 2026/04/06 13:41
DMCA 删除针对的是主机，并且是诉讼前的事情（通过 DMCA 安全港条款“我们声明 X，如果您现在删除它，您的主机就是安全的”）。如果他们升级为诉讼，那么不确定德国与美国的情况是否有显着不同。欧洲并不是因为足球联盟想要而摆脱了封锁所有 Cloudflare 之类的事情。
A DMCA takedown is targeted at the host and is a pre-lawsuit thing ("we claim X and if you take it down now your host is safe" via the DMCA Safe Harbor provisions). If they escalate to lawsuits then not sure it's significantly different in Germany vs the USA. It's not like Europe is free from things like blocking all of Cloudflare because the football league wants to.
在 HN 中查看

perching_aix 2026/04/06 11:51
在受到版权方面的法律骚扰后，搬到位于德国的所有地方的主机，在我看来并不是一个特别好的主意。当地法院不是因难以对付而臭名昭著吗？
Moving to a Germany based host of all places, after being legally harassed over copyright, doesn't strike me as a particularly good idea. Aren't the local courts infamous for being awful to deal with?
在 HN 中查看
gutley 2026/04/06 13:07
我想提醒读者不要访问列出了禁止的提取器的网站。我这样做了，并在视觉上看到了看起来像孩子的年轻女孩的卡通色情内容。

这似乎是一场针对恋童癖色情下载器的法律纠纷。

I would like to caution readers against visiting the sites that have the banned extractors listed for them. I did that and was visually accosted by cartoon pornography of young girls who look like children.

It seems that this is a legal kerfuffle over a pornography downloader for pedophiles.
